title: Traditional Medicine
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Traditional medicine refers (0) to health practices, approaches, and knowledge that have been passed down through generations in different cultures. These systems, which include Chinese medicine, Ayurveda, and indigenous healing practices, have been used for thousands of years.||Unlike modern Western medicine, which often focuses (9) on treating specific symptoms or diseases, traditional medicine typically takes a holistic approach, considering the whole person – body, mind, and spirit. Many traditional remedies are derived (10) from plants, minerals, and animal products. For centuries, traditional healers have possessed extensive knowledge about local plants and their medicinal properties.||Modern pharmaceutical companies have developed numerous drugs, (11) with many key compounds originally discovered in traditional medicine. Aspirin, for example, was derived from willow bark, (12) which had been used for centuries to relieve pain. More than a quarter of modern medicines contain ingredients that (13) were first identified in traditional remedies.||However, there are concerns about traditional medicine. The effectiveness of many treatments has (14) not been scientifically proven, and some remedies can have harmful side effects or interact dangerously with conventional medicines. The demand for certain ingredients, such (15) as rhino horn and tiger parts, has contributed to the endangerment of species.||The World Health Organization encourages countries to integrate traditional medicine into their healthcare systems (16) where appropriate, while ensuring safety, quality, and effectiveness through scientific research. The goal is to combine the best of both traditional wisdom and modern medical science.

example.text: Traditional medicine refers (0) health practices passed down through generations.

questions.explanation: Expression 'focuses on' indicates concentration on specific symptoms.
Expression 'derived from' indicates the origin of the remedies.
Preposition introducing a supplementary clause that adds information about the origin of key pharmaceutical compounds.
Relative pronoun referring to willow bark.
Simple past passive: 'were identified' in a relative clause.
Negation in present perfect: 'has not been proven'.
Expression 'such as' introduces examples of ingredients.
Conditional conjunction 'where appropriate' indicates when it is fitting.
